1.L1: Effective phishing starts before you send anything. What publicly available information about Orion Logistics employees and their communication patterns would make your pretext more convincing?
1.L2: OSINT for phishing targets: LinkedIn job titles, company domain naming conventions, recent press releases (project names, executive names), and email format guessing. The more specific your pretext, the higher the click rate.
1.L3: Use `theHarvester -d orion-logistics.local -b all` for email enumeration. Check GoPhish at 172.20.3.40 for pre-configured Orion Logistics targets. Look for a recent invoice or shipment pretext — Orion is a logistics company.

2.L1: You have a pretext and targets. What makes a phishing payload actually execute when the target opens it? Think about the difference between a payload that looks dangerous and one that gets past defenses.
2.L2: For document-based payloads: macros require user interaction (Enable Content click). HTA files execute directly. The GoPhish landing page can capture credentials without any payload at all if the pretext is convincing enough.
2.L3: Configure GoPhish: Landing page at 172.20.3.40:3333. Set your sending profile to use the Postfix server at 172.20.3.20. Use the 'Orion_Q4_Invoice' template already in GoPhish. Set tracking pixel to confirm email opens before analyzing click data.

1.L1: You've received a report of a suspicious email. What header fields tell you whether the sending domain is legitimately authorized to send email on behalf of Orion Logistics?
1.L2: Check SPF (Return-Path domain vs sending IP), DKIM (signature validation), and DMARC (policy and alignment). A spoofed email will typically fail at least one of these checks if the domain has proper email security configured.
1.L3: In the email headers: look for `Authentication-Results` header. Splunk: `index=mail sourcetype=postfix | search subject=invoice | table _time, from, to, authentication_results`. A fail on SPF + DKIM = likely spoofed. Then check if the same message reached multiple recipients.
